title: Aroldis Chapman pitching through minor injury, Garrett Whitlock’s return looms & more Red Sox pitching updates
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Aroldis Chapman has been pitching through a hamstring injury but remains effective for the Red Sox.
Garrett Whitlock is set for a rehab appearance with Worcester, eyeing a potential June 9 return to the Red Sox.
Brayan Bello was demoted to Worcester to regain form as a starter; Jake Bennett could replace him in Boston's rotation.

title: They Might Be Giants bring humor, horns, and plenty of heart to House of Blues
author: Perry Eaton
bullets: They Might Be Giants performed two marathon sets with a double encore at Boston's House of Blues.
The concert featured songs from their latest album, 'The World Is to Dig,' and spotlighted 1996's 'Factory Showroom.'
The duo was joined by an eight-piece band and included covers of Cub and Raspberries songs in the setlist.

title: The U.S. World Cup team’s biggest lineup debate has Mass. ties at its core
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Matt Freese of NYCFC and Matt Turner of New England Revolution are competing for U.S. starting goalkeeper spot.
Freese, a Harvard graduate, has had the backing of U.S. coach Mauricio Pochettino over the past year.
Ex-Revolution coach Bruce Arena suggests potential goalkeeper rotation during the World Cup, though it's unconventional.

title: New England Aquarium to celebrate World Ocean Day with free waterfront events on Sunday
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The New England Aquarium will host free World Ocean Day events on Sunday at Central Wharf, Boston, from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m.
Key attractions include 'Salt,' an inflatable humpback whale, and demonstrations by the Massachusett Tribe at Ponkapoag.
Other events include scientific talks, climate art installations, and free screenings of 'Ocean Dreams' at Simons Theatre.

title: Review & setlist: 5 Seconds of Summer keep the fans at the center of an unforgettable show
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: 5 Seconds of Summer performed at TD Garden for their 'Everyone's A Star!' World Tour on June 5, 2026.
The concert featured six acts, with fan-voted surprise song 'Red Dessert' and humorous segments enhancing the experience.
The band's interactive performance and setlist included hits like 'Youngblood' and 'Amnesia,' highlighting their evolution.

title: What 17th-century advice seekers had in common with us
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Meredith Goldstein to host advice history event at Boston Public Library on June 30 at 6 p.m.
The discussion will cover advice columns from the 1600s to present, with historian Mary Beth Norton.
Event is free and explores historical and modern relationship advice, including family and in-law issues.

title: New on Netflix and more: Top streaming picks for June 2026
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix adds movies like 'The Big Lebowski' and 'Creed' to its streaming library for June 2026.
'Hoppers' premieres on Disney+, while HBO Max debuts the award-winning film 'Pillion' in June 2026.
Apple TV adapts 'Cape Fear' into a series with Amy Adams and Javier Bardem starring.

title: Ex-Yankee Sonny Gray earns first victory against New York as member of Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray earned his first win against the Yankees with the Red Sox in a 5-3 victory at Yankee Stadium.
Gray, who previously disliked his stint with the Yankees, pitched 6.1 innings allowing eight hits and three earned runs.
Gray has a 7-1 record with a 3.20 ERA this season, contributing significantly to the Red Sox's pitching rotation.

title: Willson Contreras rides highs and lows of Red Sox win over Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a 2-run homer and a key RBI single, finishing 2 for 4 with 3 RBIs.
Contreras showed frustration with a dugout cooler after a collision with teammate Andruw Monasterio on a routine play.
Interim manager Chad Tracy praised Contreras's leadership and strong performance, with a team-leading 13 home runs.

title: Severe thunderstorms could hit Mass. Saturday evening, forecasters say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Severe thunderstorms are forecast for Massachusetts Saturday afternoon and evening with risks of hail, damaging winds, and heavy rain.
Greatest risk for severe weather is expected west and northwest of Interstate 95, potentially impacting much of Southern New England.
Severe weather might include hail causing property damage and localized flooding from heavy downpours, tapering off by 10 p.m.
